channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Tornadoes, Floods and Thunderstorms Hit U.S.
item.description: A sprawling weather system prompted flash flood alerts and tornado warnings from Kentucky up to New England on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

item.title: Colombia, el mayor falsificador de dólares del mundo
item.description: Así siguieron el rastro de uno de los mayores maestros de la falsificación de dinero del mundo.
item.dc:creator: Yudhijit Bhattacharjee

item.title: Man Who Attacked Salman Rushdie Faces Terrorism Charges in Federal Trial
item.description: Mr. Rushdie is expected to testify against Hadi Matar, the man who repeatedly stabbed him in 2022. Mr. Matar’s lawyers argue that he acted on his own, not on behalf of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

media:content.media:description: The attack on the author Salman Rushdie left him blind in his right eye.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 22, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: 遺留之物
item.description: 香港一場致命大火奪走他們摯愛的生命。數月後，他們重返宏福苑，查看火災廢墟。以下是他們從中帶回的物品。
item.dc:creator: Anjali Tsui and Lam Yik Fei

item.title: Japan Is Startled by Prime Minister’s Grueling Sleep Schedule
item.description: Sanae Takaichi has said she gets just “zero to three hours” of sleep a night, prompting a debate in the country over work-life balance.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi at a meeting at Japan’s Parliament in Tokyo last week. A social media post by Ms. Takaichi about her schedule has prompted a debate about work hours and the hazards of sleep deprivation.

item.title: ‘The Daily Show’ Tackles Trump’s Latest Move in His ‘War on Canada’
item.description: “Fighting again with Canada?” Desi Lydic said about the president imposing new tariffs on the country. “I thought we were going to work this ‘oot.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: El audaz escape de una familia de Corea del Norte
item.description: Su determinación de alcanzar la libertad se fortaleció durante la pandemia, cuando vivieron una devastadora escasez de alimentos y se vieron obligados a presenciar frecuentes ejecuciones públicas.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:credit: Woohae Cho para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kim Yu-mi, a la derecha, desertó con un grupo que incluía a sus dos hijos y a su madre. La vida en Corea del Sur no ha sido exactamente lo que imaginaban, pero no se arrepienten de haber abandonado la brutal dictadura y la incesante propaganda del Norte.

item.title: 6 Takeaways From Arizona’s Tense, Trump-Dominated Primaries
item.description: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Andy Biggs of Arizona, who was endorsed by President Trump, easily won the Republican primary for governor over Representative David Schweikert.

item.title: Iran’s Neighbors Long for a Deal, Any Deal, to End the War
item.description: Business owners in Pakistan are getting impatient for a chance to finally trade goods and oil along their long shared border with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Asim Hafeez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: Russia Will Not Yield
item.description: A journey around the globe showed the country’s relentless adaptation.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Notte

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; pool photo by Maxim Shipenkov

item.title: Quote of the Day: Lillian Roberts, Rousing leader of Municipal Union, Dies at 98
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, July 22, 2026.
Mayor Zohran Mamdani, in an interview published Saturday, described what he would do when Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu comes to New York City for the United Nations General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: The Interview

item.title: Election Denier Wins G.O.P. Nomination to Be Arizona’s Top Election Official
item.description: State Representative Alexander Kolodin will take on Adrian Fontes, the incumbent Democratic secretary of state, after defeating a more moderate Republican in the primary.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen

media:content.media:description: State Representative Alexander Kolodin at the State Capitol in Phoenix last year.

item.title: Trump Pick Wins G.O.P. Nomination to Challenge Arizona Governor
item.description: Representative Andy Biggs, a conservative Republican and an ally of the president, will try to unite Republicans against Gov. Katie Hobbs in a crucial swing state.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault

media:content.media:description: Representative Andy Biggs debated his Republican opponents in Scottsdale, Ariz., in June.

item.title: Saudis Reach Deal With U.S. That Could Let Them Enrich Nuclear Fuel
item.description: U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials have expressed opposition to such a plan, to be announced Wednesday, fearing that the kingdom could use a civilian nuclear project to develop nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Edward Wong and William J. Broad

media:content.media:description: President Trump shaking hands with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud at the White House last November.

item.title: House Narrowly Passes Stopgap Spending Bill, but Shutdown Fight Looms
item.description: Republicans managed to push through legislation to fund the government through Dec. 4. But with Democrats opposed, the stage is set for a broader battle.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson

media:content.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson at the Capitol on Tuesday.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 23, 2026
item.description: Damon Gulczynski puzzles over the mysteries of the universe.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Late-week puzzles don’t have to be scary. Ready to unmask some Thursday trickery?

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 22, 2026
item.description: Rebecca Goldstein rigs up a clever crossword.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: Wind up stuck? Read the Wordplay column for the theme explanation and other tips.

item.title: Mamdani Calls Netanyahu a ‘War Criminal’ and Calls on U.S. to Arrest Him
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani challenged the United States to execute an international arrest warrant against Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, which New York itself cannot do.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said that New York cannot arrest Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, but the United States should.

item.title: Wildfire Threatening Pleasanton Prompts Evacuation Order for Thousands
item.description: One firefighter was injured as a fire spread quickly across dry hillsides in Northern California, forcing some residents to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:description: The Little fire in Northern California, which has prompted evacuation orders.

item.title: Trump Plans to Impose Tariffs on Generic Drugs
item.description: The administration has repeatedly said it will impose steep pharmaceutical tariffs but has not followed through on them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins

media:content.media:credit: Mette Lampcov for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump said he plans to impose tariffs on generic medicines in an effort to bring pharmaceutical manufacturing back to the United States.

item.title: Zelensky Fires Ukraine’s Top General After Days of Protests
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine dismissed Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi after supporters of the ousted defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, took to the streets to call for the general’s ousting.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Inquiry Into James Dolan’s Use of Facial Recognition Ends in Modest Fines
item.description: The New York State Liquor Authority withdrew charges that tied facial recognition systems at James Dolan’s MSG Entertainment to exclusionary practices and issued $30,000 in fines for an unrelated offense.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Corey Kilgannon

media:content.media:credit: Gili Benita for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The security checkpoint through the street entrance to Madison Square Garden highlights facial recognition enforcement.

item.title: Trump Lifts Ban on Direct Flights to Lebanon
item.description: The prohibition had been in place since 1985, when it was imposed in response to Hezbollah’s hijacking of TWA Flight 847.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump with President Joseph Aoun of Lebanon at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: Tornadoes Are Reported in Kentucky as Storms Sweep Across U.S.
item.description: Storms set off tornado warnings all the way to New York on Tuesday. Forecasters also warned of flash flooding.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCann, Amy Graff, Madaleine Rubin and Francesca Regalado

item.title: Whitney Cummings’s Trump Joke at Kennedy Center Is Cut From Netflix Special
item.description: At the Mark Twain Prize ceremony, the comedian Whitney Cummings made a joke about the president that seemed to refer to Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:description: Whitney Cummings opened the ceremony for the Mark Twain Prize, which was awarded to Bill Maher at the Kennedy Center last month.

item.title: With His Latest Threats, Trump Signals to Iran That He Is Willing to Escalate
item.description: President Trump is facing a critical moment in the war, which has failed to bring about the “unconditional surrender” he once demanded.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: “We’ll be hitting that area probably pretty soon,” President Trump said of Pickaxe Mountain on Tuesday. “And there’s not a thing they can do about it.”

item.title: Hegseth Estimates Cost of Iran War Has Reached $37.5 Billion
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate Appropriations Committee Tuesday to request an additional $70 billion to fund the war and replenish weapons stockpiles.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Mykhailo Drapatyi: What to Know About Ukraine’s New Top General
item.description: Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapatyi, a decorated commander, is popular both inside and outside of his country.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:description: Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapatyi speaking to journalists in Ukraine last year.

item.title: Cyclospora Cases in the U.S. Hit Record High
item.description: Federal health officials have confirmed another 2,500 cases of the parasitic infection.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi

media:content.media:credit: Scott Rossi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Farmworkers harvesting lettuce in Holtville, Ca. last year.

item.title: Jury Deadlocks on Murder Charges Against 2 Men in California Mass Shooting
item.description: The defendants were convicted of weapons charges in connection with the 2022 shootout in Sacramento, which left six people dead and 13 others injured outside a downtown arena.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

media:content.media:description: A crime scene investigator taking photographs of evidence markers at the scene of the shooting in Sacramento, Calif., in 2022.

item.title: Former Conductor to Plead Guilty in Child Sex Abuse Imagery Case
item.description: Julian Wachner, the former music director at Trinity Church in New York, faces up to 10 years in prison after reaching a plea deal.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Julian Wachner directed the music program for Trinity Church in New York until his ouster in 2022.

item.title: Salmon With Anchovy-Garlic Butter
item.description: In this recipe from Cooking’s Melissa Clark, linked below, minced anchovies and garlic add a complex salinity to seared salmon, enriching and deepening its flavor.

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Spencer Richards.

item.title: Zelensky Removes Ukraine’s Top General
item.description: Also, the Pentagon estimates the Iran war has cost $37.5 billion. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Oleksandr Syrskyi, left, with Volodymyr Zelensky in March.

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Senate Appropriations Committee, via Associated Press

item.title: Small Plane Crashes Into House Near Houston
item.description: The plane, a Piper J3C-65, struck the house in Fulshear, Texas, around 6:45 a.m. on Monday, the authorities said. Its pilot was in serious condition on Tuesday, a hospital spokesman said.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

item.title: Hegseth Estimates Iran War Has Cost $37.5 Billion
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is requesting as much as $70 billion in emergency military spending after nearly five months of war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: “This is a new request based on new realities of a world we face, stepping up to meet that moment,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday.

item.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Went Rogue and Attacked a Digital Library
item.description: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger

media:content.media:credit: Aaron Wojack for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: OpenAI and other companies have warned that their new A.I. systems could be powerful hacking tools.

item.title: Mamdani’s Version of DOGE Tries to Put a New Spin on Efficiency
item.description: The mayor’s version has sought to include public input as it formulates changes to the New York City Charter aimed at streamlining the government.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani empaneled a Charter Revision Commission to come up with ways to improve government efficiency.

item.title: A New Critique of Trumpism Goes Too Easy on Elites
item.description: Even under populism, elite failure is still a problem.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; source images by Hirkophoto, and Scott Eisen, via Getty Images

item.title: A Wartime Rift in Ukraine
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky fired his top general after a feud between old-school officers and younger innovators spilled into the streets.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Volodymyr Zelensky and the former commander in chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, in March.

item.title: Call the Veep, Agent Mulder!
item.description: Have the “space Jews” come to get us at last?
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: What to Know About Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain
item.description: The fortified underground complex, in central Iran, is about one mile away from the Natanz uranium enrichment facility.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: Institute for Science and International Security
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A satellite image provided by Vantor shows an entrance to tunnels at Pickaxe Mountain in Iran last month.

item.title: What the Cyclospora Outbreak Reveals
item.description: Warming is expected to fuel the spread of food-borne pathogens. We might not be prepared.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown

media:content.media:description: A microscopic view of cyclospora parasite eggs, stained reddish pink, in a lab sample.

item.title: Israeli Troops Shoot Near Lebanese Army Deploying in ‘Pilot Zone’
item.description: Israeli forces fired shots near Lebanese Army troops on Tuesday as they took the first steps in a new U.S.-brokered plan to transfer control of occupied areas to Beirut.

media:content.media:description: Lebanese army troops in Zawtar al-Gharbiya, southern Lebanon, after Israeli forces withdrew from the area on Tuesday.

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Senate Appropriations Committee, via Associated Press

item.title: NYC Businesses Brace for More Flooding With Severe Storms in the Forecast
item.description: Some businesses in low-lying areas of New York City have developed routines to keep their goods and customers dry.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

item.title: Why Does Fashion Heart N.Y.?
item.description: T Magazine’s editor at large, Nick Haramis, examines why fashion designers can’t get enough of New York City.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Cousins

item.title: France Passes Law Restricting Social Media for Youths
item.description: France is the first European country to ban access to social media for anyone under 15. The law could go into effect as soon as Sept. 1.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper
